generale.title: Generale Elettrica
generale.summary: An industrial ecommerce system connected directly to the internal ERP, built to keep catalog, stock, pricing, images and shipping times in sync automatically.
overview: Generale Elettrica operates across industrial electronics, surveillance, intercom systems, climate control and installer supplies. The company had opened a large warehouse with thousands of products and a strong operational connection to the physical store.

The critical point was avoiding an ecommerce layer disconnected from daily operations. The catalog needed to run on the same data the team used every day, with no duplicated entry work and no mismatches across stock, pricing or delivery timing.
work: I integrated their internal ERP with WordPress and WooCommerce through custom APIs, building a flow that synchronizes products, stock availability, images, pricing and shipping times automatically.

I designed the ecommerce structure to support a large and highly mixed catalog, from light bulbs to surveillance gear and electrician kits. The work was not just frontend delivery; it was operational architecture between the internal system and the digital sales channel.
outcome: The result is an ecommerce platform that updates itself and mirrors the real work happening in the warehouse and store. The digital side does not need constant manual maintenance to stay aligned with the business.
That makes the project strong because it removes operational friction, keeps critical data aligned and turns the ERP into a living engine for online sales.

odora.title: Odora
odora.summary: An end-to-end digital product for fragrance discovery, built around advanced search, olfactive logic, note-based browsing and comparison of the best offers online.
overview: Odora was built as a full digital product for the fragrance space: not just a catalog, but a smart discovery tool that helps users find the right perfume through filters, olfactive families, notes and preference-based browsing.

That logic continues on the perfume detail page, where discovery meets conversion by surfacing the best available offers from stores such as Notino, Douglas, Sephora, Amazon and other relevant retailers.
work: On Odora I handled the project end-to-end: requirements, naming, logo, design system, UX, functional structure, APIs and part of the web scraping pipeline used to collect data and offers.
I shaped the product as one coherent system where brand, user experience, discovery, comparison and technical architecture support each other instead of acting like separate layers.
outcome: The strength of the project comes from the fact that perfume search does not stop at editorial content or catalog browsing. It helps users make a more precise choice and immediately see the best offer available at that moment.
Odora works because it combines product thinking, design, data and conversion in a focused, clear and recognizable experience.

balance.title: Balance
balance.summary: A privacy-first iOS app for tracking personal finances and net worth over time, built to replace complex Excel sheets with a clearer and more sustainable experience.
overview: Balance is a digital app currently available on the App Store for iOS, with Android coming next. The goal is to give users real control over their finances and net worth without forcing them into spreadsheet workflows full of formulas and maintenance.
The app supports wallet tracking, historical trends and long-term net worth visibility, while keeping a privacy-first approach and a product structure designed to last over time.
work: I defined the product experience and system direction so a typically complex topic like net worth tracking becomes cleaner, easier to read and progressively understandable.
The product logic ties together onboarding, wallet management, historical data and the freemium model: three wallets in the free plan and extended usage in the Infinity tier.
outcome: Balance is strong because it replaces improvised tools with a dedicated product designed to support the user over time instead of requiring continuous manual fixes.
Its privacy-first positioning and clarity of experience make it solid both as a daily utility and as a product ready to evolve across more platforms.
